definition: The stock-bond correlation regime is stage-dependent on AI productivity transmission. Stage 1 (current, demand-driven): ambiguous correlation. Stage 2 (competitive pricing): negative correlation (favorable for 60/40 portfolios). Current 60/40 portfolios implicitly bet on Stage 2+ outcome. Theoretically grounded but many other correlation determinants (monetary policy, fiscal, geopolitics) make AI-specific isolation difficult.
evidence: Many other correlation determinants prevent clean isolation
Rolling stock-bond correlation oscillating between -0.2 and +0.4 post-2022
5 agents suggest persistent positive correlation is plausible
Rolling 6-month stock-bond correlation: +0.15 to +0.35 since mid-2024
Three-force decomposition: AI capex demand + no TFP break + fiscal dominance
Risk parity portfolios running ~20-30% excess equity-equivalent risk
Regime is stage-dependent (could shift with AI productivity supply), not permanent
open_questions: Can the AI-specific correlation effect be isolated from monetary policy, fiscal, and geopolitical drivers?
How long does Stage 1 (ambiguous correlation) persist?
What signals the transition from Stage 1 to Stage 2?
Whether correlation regime shift is permanent or transitory
Whether fiscal dominance is sufficient condition for positive correlation
Impact of correlation regime on optimal portfolio construction
Whether AI productivity breakthrough would flip correlation back to negative
Timeline for portfolio reallocation by institutional investors
